days.title: Arrival in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Exploring the vibrant city

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel Icon
timeline.detail: Arrive at Hotel Icon, a stylish hotel conveniently located near Tsim Sha Tsui MTR station. Drop your bags and freshen up before exploring the city.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Icon, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Victoria Harbour Promenade Walk
timeline.detail: Take a relaxing stroll along the Victoria Harbour promenade. Enjoy stunning views of the skyline, especially as the sun sets. Don't forget your camera!

timeline.title: Dinner at Aqua Restaurant
timeline.detail: Dine at Aqua for a unique blend of Italian and Japanese cuisine. With breathtaking views of the harbour, it's the perfect way to kick off your trip.
timeline.mapQuery: Aqua Restaurant, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Symphony of Lights Show
timeline.detail: Watch the famous Symphony of Lights show from the waterfront. The stunning light and sound show features buildings on both sides of the harbour.

timeline.title: Street Food at Temple Street Night Market
timeline.detail: Explore the vibrant Temple Street Night Market. Try local delicacies like fish balls and fried noodles while soaking up the lively atmosphere.
timeline.mapQuery: Temple Street Night Market, Yau Ma Tei, Hong Kong

localTips: Buy an Octopus card at the airport for convenient travel on public transport.
Make dinner reservations at Aqua in advance to secure a window seat.
Visit the waterfront after dinner for the best view of the light show.

days.title: Cultural Exploration in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Discover beauty and history

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australia Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Start your day with a traditional Hong Kong breakfast at this local favorite. Try the toast and milk tea for an authentic experience.
timeline.mapQuery: Australia Dairy Company, Jordan, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Ngong Ping 360 Cable Car Ride
timeline.detail: Take the Ngong Ping 360 cable car for a breathtaking ride to Lantau Island. Enjoy panoramic views of the landscape and the South China Sea.
timeline.mapQuery: Ngong Ping 360, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Tian Tan Buddha
timeline.detail: Reach the iconic Tian Tan Buddha statue, one of the largest seated Buddha statues in the world. Explore the Po Lin Monastery nearby for a cultural experience.
timeline.mapQuery: Tian Tan Buddha, Ngong Ping, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Po Lin Monastery Vegetarian Restaurant
timeline.detail: Enjoy a delicious vegetarian meal at the restaurant within the monastery. The peaceful atmosphere enhances the dining experience.
timeline.mapQuery: Po Lin Monastery, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Explore Tai O Fishing Village
timeline.detail: Visit the charming Tai O fishing village. Ride a small boat through stilt houses and possibly spot pink dolphins in the waters.
timeline.mapQuery: Tai O Fishing Village, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner in Soho
timeline.detail: Head back to Hong Kong Island and enjoy dinner in Soho. Try some local dishes at one of the many restaurants lining the streets.

localTips: Book your Ngong Ping 360 tickets online in advance for discounts and to skip lines.
Wear comfortable shoes for walking and exploring Tai O village.
Try the vegan dumplings at Po Lin Monastery.

days.title: Shopping and Departure
days.subtitle: Last-minute shopping and food

timeline.title: Breakfast at DimDimSum Dim Sum Specialty Store
timeline.detail: Savor some of the best dim sum in Hong Kong. Be sure to try the siu mai and har gow for a true taste of local cuisine.
timeline.mapQuery: DimDimSum Dim Sum Specialty Store, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Shopping at Mong Kok
timeline.detail: Explore the bustling streets of Mong Kok, famous for its markets and shops. Visit the Ladies Market for souvenirs and trendy clothing.

timeline.title: Lunch at Mak's Noodle
timeline.detail: Try the legendary wonton noodles at Mak's Noodle, which has been serving locals for decades. The delicate noodles and shrimp wontons are unforgettable.

timeline.title: Visit Man Mo Temple
timeline.detail: Stop by the historic Man Mo Temple, dedicated to the gods of literature and war. The intricate details and incense-filled air create a tranquil atmosphere.

timeline.title: Head to Airport
timeline.detail: Leave for Hong Kong International Airport with plenty of time to spare. Enjoy the high-speed train ride to the airport, reflecting on your wonderful trip.

localTips: Mong Kok can get crowded; visit during the week for a quieter experience.
Consider getting additional snacks from local shops for the flight home.
Say goodbye to Hong Kong with a final stroll along Tsim Sha Tsui waterfront.

hotels.name: Hotel Icon
hotels.note: 5-min walk from Tsim Sha Tsui MTR, luxury with harbor views.
hotels.address: 17 Science Museum Rd, Tsim Sha Tsui, Kowloon, Hong Kong

budget.note: Round trip tickets via low-cost carrier.

urgent.note: Tickets sell out quickly, book online.
